summary: The case now has a compact final bounded verdict: instrumentation-first is supported, while strong closed-loop or durable-improvement claims remain unsupported.

steps.name: Capture trigger evidence
available_inputs: review request contains alert metrics (task_type null, Uses 5, Avg Quality 0.500, Success Rate 0%, Last Seen 2026-03-22 15:08:49)
real-case vault note summarizes the same signal
supporting rationale exists in selection note
missing_inputs: raw dashboard or database snapshot reference

steps.name: Run review and classify the case
available_inputs: review artifact exists
response artifact exists
post-review evaluation exists
classification already resolved to instrumentation-focused Fall C
missing_inputs: formal mapping of reviewer identity into the proof-case object

steps.name: Define one minimal reversible intervention
available_inputs: candidate intervention direction is clear: task_type/outcome-consistency gate before regression judgment
single intervention statement is now selected
approval owner is known
rollback refs are known

steps.name: Implement and log the runtime change
available_inputs: canonical implementation/change surface is known: skill_signal_gates.py + generate_regression_draft_from_db.py
retrospective change record is now explicit
activation commit/time is grounded in git history
affected metrics/views are named
rollback reference is named

steps.name: Measure bounded post-change effect
available_inputs: baseline comparator is defined as same-snapshot ungated vs gated comparison
observation window is defined and executed as one bounded live verification run
minimum threshold and disconfirmation rule are explicit
initial bounded observations exist
initial verdict posture is recorded
missing_inputs: repeated observation window or broader downstream evidence
stronger post-change evidence for sustained review-quality improvement

actions: decide whether the bounded measurement is enough for the local proof-case verdict
if stronger confidence is desired, collect repeated candidate-emission checks over a defined window
document whether downstream review workload quality changes measurably
